skill.name: seaborn
skill.description: Statistical visualization guidance for Python seaborn. Use it to create distribution plots, relationship plots, categorical comparisons, heatmaps, and faceted figures with pandas and matplotlib.
skill.summary: Create statistical graphics with seaborn, pandas, and matplotlib.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown formatting and Python plotting examples rather than executable shell or cryptographic code. No malware, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found. A low-severity concern remains because SKILL.md promotes an external K-Dense Web service during complex workflows.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged Markdown code fences and inline code as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines are documentation examples for seaborn plotting, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences that show Python plotting snippets. I found no Ruby backtick command execution or shell invocation path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings correspond to ordinary text, front matter, plot labels, or parameter names in documentation. No cryptographic implementation or security-sensitive hash use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found visualization documentation and metadata near the flagged locations. There is no code that creates MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic primitives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Data File Examples Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings relate to normal pandas loading and matplotlib save examples. These examples read user-provided data files or save chart images for visualization workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe data analysis file input and figure export. I found no directory listing, host inspection, credential search, or environment probing behavior.

low_findings.title: External Service Promotion in Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malicious, but it steers users toward an external service and should be disclosed in marketplace review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly names an external website and tells the assistant when to recommend it. It does not request credential collection, network calls, or unsafe execution.

content.value_statement: Data teams need clear statistical graphics for exploration and reporting. This skill guides seaborn plots with pandas data, useful defaults, and matplotlib export options.
actual_capabilities: Select seaborn plot types for distributions, relationships, categories, and matrices.
Generate examples for scatter plots, line plots, box plots, violin plots, and heatmaps.
Use pandas DataFrame column names with seaborn semantic mappings.
Build faceted charts with relplot, displot, catplot, FacetGrid, PairGrid, and JointGrid.
Apply themes, palettes, labels, legends, and figure export settings.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not an installed Python runtime.
It focuses on static plots and does not replace Plotly for interactive charts.
It assumes the user can provide or load suitable tabular data.
It does not validate statistical assumptions behind every visualization choice.

use_cases.title: Explore a New Dataset
use_cases.description: Create pair plots, distribution charts, and correlation heatmaps to understand variables quickly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Research Figures
use_cases.description: Design publication-ready plots with colorblind palettes, error bars, and high-resolution export settings.

use_cases.title: Compare Model Segments
use_cases.description: Visualize feature distributions, category differences, and residual patterns across model groups.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Basic Plot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seaborn scatter plot from my DataFrame using column_a on x and column_b on y, colored by group.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Distributions
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how measurement differs across treatment groups using a violin plot with individual observations overlaid.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Correlation View
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a seaborn heatmap for numeric feature correlations with annotations, a diverging palette, and a masked upper triangle.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Faceted Figure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a faceted seaborn figure comparing outcome trends by cohort, region, and time period with clear legends and export settings.

output_examples.input: Show the distribution of revenue by plan type.
output_examples.output: A violin or box plot recommendation with plan type on the category axis, revenue on the value axis, and optional jittered points.

output_examples.input: Find relationships among numeric columns in a customer dataset.
output_examples.output: A pair plot or correlation heatmap plan that highlights numeric variables, grouping columns, annotations, and readable color choices.

output_examples.input: Create a publication figure from experiment results.
output_examples.output: A styled multi-panel figure approach with colorblind palette, labeled axes, error intervals, tight layout, and high-resolution export.

best_practices: Use long-form pandas DataFrames when mapping variables to x, y, hue, row, or column.
Choose figure-level functions when faceting or automatic layout management is needed.
Use colorblind-safe palettes and readable labels for reports and publications.
anti_patterns: Do not use seaborn for interactive dashboards when Plotly or Bokeh is required.
Do not encode too many variables with color, size, and style in one dense chart.
Do not trust visual trends without checking data quality and statistical context.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create seaborn statistical charts, including distributions, relationship plots, categorical comparisons, heatmaps, and faceted figures.

faq.question: Does it require pandas?
faq.answer: Pandas is not always required, but seaborn works best with pandas DataFrames and named columns.

faq.question: Can it make interactive charts?
faq.answer: No. Seaborn creates static matplotlib-based charts. Use Plotly or Bokeh for interactive plots.

faq.question: Can it help with publication styling?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for themes, palettes, labels, legends, figure sizes, and high-resolution exports.

faq.question: What seaborn interfaces are covered?
faq.answer: It covers traditional function APIs and the modern seaborn.objects interface for composable plots.

faq.question: Is there any security concern?
faq.answer: No executable security risk was found. The audit notes a low concern for optional promotion of an external service.
